commands.review_summary:
The branch adds a one-shot Codex app-server `onBlockReply` pre-tool announcement for native and dynamic tools, suppresses messaging-tool announcements, marks the announcement replay-unsafe, and adds focused ordering/copy tests.

Reproducibility: not applicable. as a strict bug reproduction: this PR adds a new Codex chat UX behavior. Source inspection of current main shows no same-medium pre-tool plan reply, and the PR's reproduction signal is targeted harness coverage rather than a failing current-main user path.
commands.review_followup: Needs contributor or Mantis real behavior proof and maintainer UX approval for the extra automatic chat reply; there is no narrow ClawSweeper code repair to queue.

target.title: Announce tool plans in chat replies
target.body:
## Summary

- Move the pre-tool plan announcement into the Codex app-server runtime, not the generic embedded pi subscription layer.
- Send one concise same-medium `onBlockReply` announcement before the first Codex-native tool progress event or OpenClaw dynamic tool execution, then immediately continue the tool path.
- Keep messaging tools suppressed, flush the announcement when the channel provides a flusher, and mark the visible announcement as a replay-relevant side effect.

## Root Cause

Codex app-server runs do not enter the generic `src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.handlers.tools.ts` `tool_execution_start` path for the Codex-native tool lifecycle, and OpenClaw dynamic tools in Codex are handled from the app-server `item/tool/call` request boundary. The correct ownership is therefore the Codex runtime surface in `extensions/codex/src/app-server/event-projector.ts` for native Codex tool items, and `extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.ts` for dynamic OpenClaw tool requests. The prior generic pi-hook approach would have missed Codex-native app-server tools and would have applied policy outside the requested runtime boundary.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: A chat message handled by the Codex runtime that reaches tool use now first emits a concise plan announcement through the same block-reply chat medium, flushes it when supported, then proceeds directly into tool progress or tool execution.

Real environment tested: Temporary OpenClaw worktree under `/tmp`, branch `tool-call-plan-chat-reply`, Node via repo wrappers.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/app-server/event-projector.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose`

Evidence after fix: `Test Files  2 passed (2)` and `Tests  246 passed (246)`.

Observed result after fix: The Codex native tool test observes `onBlockReply` receiving `I'll run the needed command with Codex tools, then report the result here.`, then `onBlockReplyFlush`, then tool progress. The dynamic OpenClaw tool test observes `I'll use Codex tools to handle this, then report the result here.` before `tool_execution_started`. The copy test rejects the vague `continue with the request` wording and enforces two sentences max.

What was not tested: Live Telegram forum-topic round trip was not run; this change intentionally uses the existing same-medium `onBlockReply` delivery callback, so Telegram group/topic routing stays on the already-selected chat medium rather than introducing a separate channel path. Broad remote Testbox/Crabbox proof was previously blocked because Testbox warmup did not return a `tbx_` id and direct AWS Crabbox could not refresh credentials from EC2 IMDS in this environment.

## What was not tested

- Live Telegram group/forum-topic round trip.
- Broad remote Testbox/Crabbox proof, blocked as described above.
- Non-Codex runtimes, by design; this PR is scoped to the Codex app-server runtime.

## Verification

- `pnpm exec oxfmt --write --threads=1 extensions/codex/src/app-server/event-projector.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/event-projector.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/app-server/event-projector.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose` -> `Test Files  2 passed (2)`, `Tests  246 passed (246)`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.test.src.json --incremental false --pretty false`
- `git diff --check`
- `pnpm changed:lanes --json` -> `extensions: true`, `extensionTests: true`, all core/app/docs/tooling lanes false
- `AUTOREVIEW_AUTO_TESTS=0 .agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch` -> `autoreview clean: no accepted/actionable findings reported`
